title: Seed
description: A complete, self-contained case definition for one deterministic vulnerability insertion run. The seed bundles the integer seed, the target vulnerability class, the mutation strategy, and target-selection constraints. Given a seed file and a source tree, the pipeline is fully determined.

seed_id.description: Stable, human-readable identifier for this seed definition. Should be unique within a corpus.

seed.description: Integer seed. Passed directly to random.seed(). Fully determines target ordering given a fixed source tree and spec.

cwe_id.description: CWE identifier for the vulnerability class to insert, e.g. 'CWE-122'.

vulnerability_class.description: Human-readable name for the vulnerability class, e.g. 'Heap-based Buffer Overflow'.

description.description: Optional plain-text description of what this seed is intended to produce.

mutation_strategy.description: Strategy identifier used by the Patcher. Must correspond to a registered mutation strategy in the pipeline.

target_pattern.description: Constraints on which code locations are eligible targets for this seed.

pattern_type.description: Primary pattern type the Seeder uses to identify candidates.

custom_pattern_id.description: Required when pattern_type is 'custom'. References an externally registered pattern.

constraints.description: Pattern-specific constraints to narrow candidate selection.

require_function_scope.description: If true, targets must be inside a function body (not at file/global scope).

min_candidate_score.description: Minimum plausibility score a candidate must reach to be selected (0.0–1.0).

source_constraints.description: Optional constraints on which source files are considered for target selection.

file_patterns.description: Glob patterns for source files to include. Default: all .c, .cpp, .cc, .h, .hpp.

exclude_patterns.description: Glob patterns for source files to exclude (e.g. test files).

max_targets.description: Maximum number of patch targets to select for this run.

metadata.description: Optional human-facing metadata. Does not affect pipeline behaviour.

difficulty.description: Subjective difficulty rating for a detector to find this vulnerability.

references.description: External references (CVE IDs, paper titles, URLs).
